body:
verification_command=rg -n 'sticky route key|Endpoint utilization|AC-FEAT-004-16|AC-FEAT-004-17|AC-FEAT-004-18' docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md && rg -n 'sticky local endpoint assignment|vllm:num_requests_running|llama-server' docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-005-smart-routing-replaces-model-routes.md && rg -n 'fuzzy-match|AC-FEAT-004-14' docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md
exit_code=0
output:
72:- **Endpoint utilization** — a normalized operational signal for one provider
241:     model at equivalent quality, routing assigns each new sticky route key to
244:     Requests with the same sticky route key reuse the assigned endpoint to keep
379:| AC-FEAT-004-16 | For equivalent local endpoints serving the same resolved model, the same sticky route key repeatedly resolves to the same provider endpoint until its lease expires or the endpoint becomes unavailable. | `go test ./internal/routing ./... -run 'Sticky|Lease|Routing'` |
380:| AC-FEAT-004-17 | Different sticky route keys are distributed across equivalent local endpoints by normalized load; a saturated endpoint is avoided for new keys, while an existing key stays pinned unless the endpoint is unavailable or hard-saturated. | `go test ./internal/routing ./... -run 'Utilization|Sticky|RouteStatus'` |
381:| AC-FEAT-004-18 | vLLM and llama-server utilization probes feed normalized endpoint utilization into routing; stale or failed probes fall back to service-owned in-flight lease counts without making otherwise healthy endpoints unavailable. | `go test ./internal/provider/... ./internal/routing/... -run 'Utilization|VLLM|Llama|Routing'` |
124:vLLM, and llama-server providers are treated as free marginal cost but still
160:`vllm:num_requests_running`, `vllm:num_requests_waiting`, and cache pressure.
161:`llama-server` probes root `/metrics` when started with `--metrics`, and falls
217:5. **Apply sticky local endpoint assignment**: reuse an existing live lease for
377:| AC-FEAT-004-14 | Provider-native model IDs with case, vendor-prefix, quantization, accelerator, or packaging suffix differences, such as `Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit`, fuzzy-match to the intended catalog model for power, context, tool support, and auto-routable metadata without treating unrelated short names as equivalent. | `go test ./internal/modelcatalog ./internal/routing ./... -run 'Fuzzy|ProviderNative|ModelEligibility'` |

verification_command passed: rg -n 'sticky route key|Endpoint utilization|AC-FEAT-004-16|AC-FEAT-004-17|AC-FEAT-004-18' docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md && rg -n 'sticky local endpoint assignment|vllm:num_requests_running|llama-server' docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-005-smart-routing-replaces-model-routes.md && rg -n 'fuzzy-match|AC-FEAT-004-14' docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md
